Services

## One wall. Three ways to raise it.

Run HEIMWALL wherever your infrastructure lives — in your own virtualization environment, on our cloud, or as dedicated hardware.

### HEIMWALL Cloud

HEIMWALL delivered over the Cloud — point your DNS and you're protected in minutes, no installation. [...] Why Heimwall

## Three reasons teams pick HEIMWALL.

Most walls look the same on paper. Here is where HEIMWALL is genuinely different.

### Your data stays in your country.

HEIMWALL runs PoPs in Turkey with a KVKK/GDPR-aligned architecture. And with the Hosted model, traffic inspection happens entirely on your own servers — your data never has to leave your environment.

### Sell it as your own.

The white-label reseller model is rare among edge providers. Onboard, manage and bill your own customers from a branded console — HEIMWALL runs underneath, your brand is what they see.

### One platform, one console.

WAF, CDN and managed DNS — with geo-routing, automatic failover and live monitoring — in a single console. No stitching three separate vendors together.

See all features [...] See all features

Request a demo

## See the wall hold, live.

Get a guided tour of the HEIMWALL console with your own traffic scenarios.

Request Demo Contact Sales

results.title: Why We Keep Buying Broken Games (And How It Got This Bad)
results.content:
broken launches shocked us. like Assassin's Creed Unity with missing faces and game-breaking bugs, or Battlefield 4, so unstable that DICE had to pause development of their other projects just to fix it. But over time, the outrage faded because there was always another broken launch right after it. Mass Effect Andromeda, Fallout 76, No Man's Sky, Cyberpunk, Starfield, Anthem, Dragon Age, the list goes on and on. Each disaster normalized by the idea that launch issues are just part of the process. And the marketing departments, they were smart. They stopped calling it broken. They just called it a live [music] service. Next, of course, came the beta excuse. The most effective rebranding of unfinished product ever. Today, open beta means we're launching next week. It's no longer about [...] and disc was it. Sure, you might see a patch later, but it wasn't essential. The game was basically playable, and any bugs were just considered features or janky charm. Then came the internet and the slow normalization of post-launch fixes. It started harmlessly enough with, of course, Bethesda's Oblivion Horse Armor DLC in 2006. It's minor, cosmetic, forgettable, right? But soon, the model evolved. By the late 2000s and early 2010s, studios realized, why spend another 6 months polishing when you can release the game now and patch it later while still getting the full price from the consumer. And the gamers eager to play these games just let them. The first step in our collective brainwashing was desensitization. [music] At first, broken launches shocked us. like Assassin's Creed Unity [...] 10 comments

results.title: Firewalla Reviews 2026 → (5.0/5) Smartest Firewall Across 100+ Countries
results.content:
Can Firewalla remove negative reviews?    

Firewalla cannot remove reviews just because they are negative or critical. However, the merchant can report reviews that look fake or break our guidelines (for example, clearly invented orders or abusive content). When that happens, MyProsAndCons reviews the case, may ask the reviewer for extra documentation, and can remove the review if the experience cannot be reasonably supported.

    What should I mention in a review if I had a problem with Firewalla ? [...] We combine human moderation with automated checks to detect unusual patterns, fake accounts, and content that breaks our rules related to Firewalla reviews. When necessary, we may hide or remove reviews, or place Firewalla under extra checks.

    Fresh, relevant feedback about Firewalla first    

Our default sorting aims to highlight recent and relevant feedback so buyers can see what others experienced with Firewalla in the latest months, not just years ago.

    Constructive feedback about Firewalla, not personal attacks    

We encourage reviewers to focus on what actually happened with Firewalla: what they ordered, what went wrong or right, and how it was handled. We do not allow hate, threats, or personal attacks on people.

    Extra checks for Firewalla reviews when needed [...] The customer was happy and realized he was spending money on the right investment. He was very pleased with the outstanding performance of a small box.

Another customer was amazed by the functionality of the small box. He got all the complex features he was used to with great flexibility.

> I love the box! Only day 2 of use, and I am already in love with it. Love the intricate features I can use. I love the options and flexibility.

The next review came from a customer who left a comment on Kickstarter. He said:

> Firewalla is badass. It was very fast and simple to install. So glad I got an alert when I was away from home!

The easy and fast installation made the customer very happy. He was even happy that he was still getting alerts despite being away from home.
